markdownRemark.html:
Preface

  • Some properties in components and DataSet Field (hereinafter referred to as Field) have the same name and serve the same function. When both the component and Field are set, there may be issues regarding property priority and whether they take effect.

  • The following principles apply:

    1. For display-type properties with the same name, the component has higher priority.

      1. Example: help

    2. For validation-type properties with the same name, Field has higher priority because data validation related to the component is performed in the DataSet after association; there are some special cases: when a component is associated with a DataSet, the property set on the component is invalid (even if the corresponding Field in the DataSet has not set this property).

      1. Example: validator, required
    3. For other-type properties with the same name, generally those related to data have higher priority in Field.

Below is an explanation of the priority when Field and component have the same property set at the same time:

Display-type

label

  • Components: Components within Form children
  • Priority: Component > Field

labelWidth

  • Components: Form and components within Form children
  • Priority: (Take the maximum value from Form and components within Form children) > Field

numberGrouping, formatterOptions

  • Components: NumberField, Currency
  • Priority: Component > Field

highlight

  • Components: Components with this property
  • Priority: Component > Field

help

  • Components: Inherited components of FormField
  • Priority: Component > Field

readOnly, disabled

  • Components: Inherited components of FormField
  • Priority: If either the component or Field is true, then it is true

Validation

pattern

  • Components: TextField and components inherited from TextField
  • Priority: Only valid on Field
  • Explanation: When the component is associated with a DataSet, setting this property on the component is invalid (even if it is not set on the Field in DataSet), because it belongs to validation properties

maxLength

  • Components: TextField and components inherited from TextField
  • Priority: Field > Component
  • Explanation: Setting maxLength on the component will restrict input; setting it on the Field will restrict input and perform validation

minLength

  • Components: TextField and components inherited from TextField
  • Priority: Only valid on Field
  • Explanation: When the component is associated with a DataSet, setting this property on the component is invalid (even if it is not set on the Field in DataSet), because it belongs to validation properties

max, min

  • Components: NumberField, Currency, DatePicker
  • Priority: Only valid on Field
  • Explanation: When the component is associated with a DataSet, setting this property on the component is invalid (even if it is not set on the Field in DataSet), because it belongs to validation properties

step, nonStrictStep

  • Components: NumberField, Currency, DatePicker
  • Priority: Only effective for Field
  • Explanation: When the component is associated with a DataSet, this property set on the component is invalid (even if the Field on the DataSet does not have this property set), because it is used for data validation.

precision

  • Components: NumberField, Currency
  • Priority: Field > Component

range

  • Components: NumberField, Currency
  • Priority: Only effective for Field
  • Explanation: When the component is associated with a DataSet, this property set on the component is invalid (even if the Field on the DataSet does not have this property set), because it is used for data validation.

validator

  • Components: Components that inherit from FormField
  • Priority: Field > Component

required

  • Components: Components that inherit from FormField
  • Priority: Only effective for Field
  • Explanation: When the component is associated with a DataSet, this property set on the component is invalid (even if the Field on the DataSet does not have this property set), because it is a validation property.

Other Types

defaultValue

  • Components: Components that have this property
  • Priority: Only effective for Field
  • Explanation: When the component is associated with a DataSet, this property set on the component is invalid (even if the Field on the DataSet does not have this property set), because data initialization happens in the DataSet.

trim

  • Components: Components that inherit from FormField
  • Priority: Only effective for Field
  • Explanation: When the component is associated with a DataSet, this property set on the component is invalid (even if the Field on the DataSet does not have this property set), because data management is handled in the DataSet.

multiple

  • Components: Components that have this property
  • Priority: Field > Component

showCheckedStrategy

  • Components: Lov, TreeSelect
  • Priority: Field > Component

processValue

  • Components: Components that inherit from FormField
  • Priority: Field > Component
